steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The speaker says "there is no better example of the form" — meaning no one has surpassed Pinkney's illustrations. The blank needs a word meaning to surpass or outshine someone's work.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — cited: means to reference or mention as an example. Saying no one has "cited" Pinkney makes no sense in context — it doesn't express being surpassed. ✗
  • Choice B — eclipsed: means to surpass or outshine. "No one has ever eclipsed Pinkney's work" directly matches the speaker's claim that his work remains the best example of the form. ✓
  • Choice C — augmented: means to add to or increase. Saying no one has "augmented" his work would mean no one has built on it — not the intended meaning. ✗
  • Choice D — convened: means to assemble or call together a group. Completely unrelated to the idea of surpassing artistic work. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
